title: calydorea basaltica
intro: Calydorea basaltica, also known as the 'Basalt Calydorea', is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its roots and tubers are used to treat various ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calydorea basaltica include treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and tubers are often dried and ground into powder or made into teas and infusions, which can be consumed for their medicinal properties.
